one_line: Founder-king of Vijayanagara from the Hoysala–Kampili world in 14th‑century South India.

appearance.face: oval face, medium brown South Indian complexion, straight nose, neatly trimmed moustache with faint beard along jawline, sharp, focused eyes; default expression calm but intense, projecting strategic confidence
appearance.hair: thick black hair gathered into a short, tidy knot at the back of the head, front gently parted and smoothed; simple golden headband circling the forehead with a small leaf-shaped ornament at the center
appearance.costume: a knee-length cream-colored cotton angarkha-style tunic with side ties and a modest round neckline, worn over a deep maroon dhoti wrapped firmly to the ankles in the South Indian style, bordered with a thin gold stripe; over this, a sleeveless dark green quilted cloth-armor vest with subtle diamond stitching, fitted but not bulky; a rich saffron-ochre cotton shawl draped from the left shoulder across the chest and around the back; simple leather sandals; modest gold stud earrings, a thin gold chain with a small tulasi-leaf pendant, and two plain gold bangles on each wrist
appearance.signature: a steel straight-edged sword with a gently tapering single-edged blade and a South Indian-style round pommel, carried sheathed at his left hip in a dark brown leather scabbard with simple brass fittings and held in his right hand when drawn

ref_desc: Harihara I has an oval medium-brown face with a neat moustache, short-knotted black hair and gold headband, cream tunic, maroon dhoti, dark green quilted vest, saffron-ochre shawl, simple gold jewelry, and a sheathed steel sword with brown scabbard and brass fittings.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style character of Harihara I as a mid-30s South Indian king with an oval medium-brown face, sharp intense eyes, straight nose, and a neatly trimmed moustache with faint beard, wearing short-knotted black hair tied at the back and a simple gold headband with a small central leaf ornament. Show him in a knee-length cream cotton angarkha-style tunic over a deep maroon ankle-length South Indian dhoti with a thin gold border, covered by a fitted sleeveless dark green quilted cloth-armor vest with diamond stitching. Drape a rich saffron-ochre shawl from his left shoulder across his chest and around his back, add modest gold stud earrings, a thin gold chain with a tiny tulasi-leaf pendant, and two plain gold bangles on each wrist. Put simple leather sandals on his feet. At his left hip show a steel straight-edged sword with a gently tapering single-edged blade, in a dark brown leather scabbard with brass fittings; in some poses he lightly rests his right hand on the hilt. Use cream, maroon, dark green, saffron-ochre, and gold as the dominant colors and present him in a clean model-sheet style that clearly shows face, hair, clothing details, and sword design from head to toe.
